title: Differential Equations
quote: He who seeks for methods without having a definite problem in mind seeks for the most part in vain. - D. HILBERT

sections.title: Introduction
concepts: A differential equation is an equation involving derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to independent variable(s).
For a given function g, find a function f such that dy/dx = g(x), where y = f(x). An equation of this form is known as a differential equation.
Differential equations arise in Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Anthropology, Geology, Economics etc.
In this chapter: basic concepts, general and particular solutions, formation of differential equations, methods to solve first order first degree differential equations, and applications.
key_points: An equation involving derivative(s) of the dependent variable with respect to independent variable(s) is called a differential equation.
x(dy/dx) + y = 0 is a differential equation because it involves a derivative of y with respect to x.
An ordinary differential equation involves derivatives with respect to only one independent variable.
2(d²y/dx²) + (dy/dx)³ = 0 is an ordinary differential equation.
Partial differential equations involve derivatives with respect to more than one independent variable (not covered in this chapter).

sections.title: Basic Concepts
concepts: Equations like x² - 3x + 3 = 0, sin x + cos x = 0, x + y = 7 involve independent and/or dependent variables only.
Equation x(dy/dx) + y = 0 involves variables as well as derivative - this is a differential equation.
key_points: Notation: dy/dx = y', d²y/dx² = y'', d³y/dx³ = y'''
For higher order derivatives: yₙ denotes dⁿy/dxⁿ (nth order derivative)

sections.title: Order of a Differential Equation
concepts: Order of a differential equation is defined as the order of the highest order derivative of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variable involved in the given differential equation.

definitions.definition: The order of the highest order derivative of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variable involved in the given differential equation.

key_points: dy/dx = eˣ has order 1 (highest derivative is first order)
d²y/dx² + y = 0 has order 2 (highest derivative is second order)
(d³y/dx³) + x²(d²y/dx²)³ = 0 has order 3 (highest derivative is third order)
Order is always a positive integer.

sections.title: Degree of a Differential Equation
concepts: The differential equation must be a polynomial equation in its derivatives (y', y'', y''' etc.) for degree to be defined.
Degree is the highest power (positive integral index) of the highest order derivative in the differential equation.

definitions.definition: When a differential equation is a polynomial equation in derivatives, the degree is the highest power (positive integral index) of the highest order derivative involved in the given differential equation.

key_points: d³y/dx³ + 2(d²y/dx²)² - dy/dx + y = 0: order 3, degree 1 (polynomial in y''', y'', y'; highest power of y''' is 1)
(dy/dx)² + (dy/dx) - sin²y = 0: order 1, degree 2 (polynomial in y'; highest power of y' is 2)
dy/dx + sin(dy/dx) = 0: order 1, degree NOT DEFINED (not a polynomial equation in y' due to sin(dy/dx))
Order and degree (if defined) are always positive integers.
Degree is not defined when the differential equation is not a polynomial equation in its derivatives.

sections.title: General and Particular Solutions of a Differential Equation
concepts: Solution of a differential equation is a function φ that satisfies the equation when substituted for the dependent variable.
The curve y = φ(x) is called the solution curve (integral curve) of the differential equation.
General solution (primitive) contains arbitrary constants.
Particular solution is obtained from general solution by giving particular values to the arbitrary constants.

definitions.definition: The solution which contains arbitrary constants is called the general solution (primitive) of the differential equation. It contains as many arbitrary constants as the order of the equation.

The solution free from arbitrary constants, i.e., the solution obtained from the general solution by giving particular values to the arbitrary constants, is called a particular solution of the differential equation.

key_points: y = a sin(x + b) is the general solution of d²y/dx² + y = 0 (contains two arbitrary constants a, b).
y = 2 sin(x + π/4) is a particular solution of d²y/dx² + y = 0 (specific values a=2, b=π/4).
The number of arbitrary constants in the general solution equals the order of the differential equation.
A particular solution has zero arbitrary constants.
Verification: substitute the function and its derivatives into the equation; if LHS = RHS, it is a solution.

sections.title: Methods of Solving First Order, First Degree Differential Equations
concepts: Three methods discussed: (1) Variable separable, (2) Homogeneous equations, (3) Linear differential equations.

sections.title: Differential Equations with Variables Separable
concepts: A first order-first degree differential equation is of the form dy/dx = F(x, y).
If F(x, y) can be expressed as a product g(x)·h(y), where g(x) is a function of x and h(y) is a function of y, then the equation is of variable separable type.
The equation becomes: dy/dx = h(y)·g(x).
If h(y) ≠ 0, separating variables: (1/h(y)) dy = g(x) dx.
Integrating both sides: ∫(1/h(y)) dy = ∫g(x) dx gives H(y) = G(x) + C.

definitions.definition: A method of solving first order differential equations where dy/dx = F(x,y) can be written as dy/dx = g(x)·h(y), allowing separation of variables so that all terms involving y are on one side and all terms involving x are on the other side.

formulas.name: Variable Separable Form
formulas.description: Standard form for variable separable equations

formulas.name: General Solution
formulas.description: Integrate both sides to get the solution; H(y) = G(x) + C

key_points: Step 1: Express dy/dx as a product of a function of x and a function of y.
Step 2: Separate variables - put all y terms with dy on one side and all x terms with dx on the other.
Step 3: Integrate both sides.
Step 4: Apply initial conditions (if given) to find particular solution.
Example: dy/dx = (x+1)/(2-y), (y≠2). Separating: (2-y)dy = (x+1)dx. Solution: x² + y² + 2x - 4y + C = 0.
Example: dy/dx = (1+y²)/(1+x²). Separating: dy/(1+y²) = dx/(1+x²). Solution: tan⁻¹y = tan⁻¹x + C.

sections.title: Homogeneous Differential Equations
concepts: A function F(x, y) is said to be a homogeneous function of degree n if F(λx, λy) = λⁿF(x, y) for any nonzero constant λ.
A homogeneous function F(x, y) of degree n can be written as F(x, y) = xⁿg(y/x) or yⁿh(x/y).
A differential equation dy/dx = F(x, y) is said to be homogeneous if F(x, y) is a homogeneous function of degree zero.
To solve: substitute y = vx (where v is a function of x), then dy/dx = v + x(dv/dx).
This reduces the equation to variable separable form in v and x.

definitions.definition: A function F(x, y) is said to be a homogeneous function of degree n if F(λx, λy) = λⁿF(x, y) for any nonzero constant λ.
A differential equation of the form dy/dx = F(x, y) is said to be homogeneous if F(x, y) is a homogeneous function of degree zero.

formulas.name: Reduced form
formulas.description: After substitution, separate variables in v and x

formulas.name: General Solution
formulas.description: Integrate and replace v by y/x to get the solution

key_points: F₁(x,y) = y² + 2xy is homogeneous of degree 2 (F₁(λx,λy) = λ²F₁).
F₂(x,y) = 2x - 3y is homogeneous of degree 1 (F₂(λx,λy) = λF₂).
F₃(x,y) = cos(y/x) is homogeneous of degree 0 (F₃(λx,λy) = λ⁰F₃).
F₄(x,y) = sin x + cos y is NOT a homogeneous function.
If the equation is in the form dx/dy = F(x,y) where F is homogeneous of degree zero, substitute x = vy.
After substitution and integration, always replace v back by y/x (or x/y).
Example: (x-y)dy/dx = x + 2y. Let y = vx. Solution: log|(x² + xy + y²)| = 2√3 tan⁻¹((x+2y)/(√3·x)) + C.

sections.title: Linear Differential Equations
concepts: A first order linear differential equation has the form dy/dx + Py = Q, where P and Q are constants or functions of x only.
Another form: dx/dy + P₁x = Q₁, where P₁ and Q₁ are constants or functions of y only.
Solved using an Integrating Factor (I.F.).
The I.F. is chosen so that multiplying the equation by I.F. makes the LHS an exact derivative.

definitions.definition: A differential equation of the form dy/dx + Py = Q, where P and Q are constants or functions of x only.
The function g(x) = e^(∫P dx) which when multiplied to both sides of the linear differential equation dy/dx + Py = Q, makes the LHS the derivative of y·e^(∫P dx). It is denoted as I.F.

formulas.name: Standard Form (Type 1)
formulas.description: P, Q are constants or functions of x only

formulas.name: Integrating Factor (Type 1)
formulas.description: Integrating factor for dy/dx + Py = Q

formulas.name: General Solution (Type 1)
formulas.description: y · e^(∫P dx) = ∫(Q · e^(∫P dx)) dx + C

formulas.name: Standard Form (Type 2)
formulas.description: P₁, Q₁ are constants or functions of y only

formulas.name: Integrating Factor (Type 2)
formulas.description: Integrating factor for dx/dy + P₁x = Q₁

formulas.name: General Solution (Type 2)
formulas.description: x · e^(∫P₁ dy) = ∫(Q₁ · e^(∫P₁ dy)) dy + C

key_points: Steps to solve dy/dx + Py = Q: (i) Write in standard form, (ii) Find I.F. = e^(∫P dx), (iii) Solution: y·(I.F.) = ∫(Q × I.F.)dx + C.
Steps to solve dx/dy + P₁x = Q₁: (i) Write in standard form, (ii) Find I.F. = e^(∫P₁ dy), (iii) Solution: x·(I.F.) = ∫(Q₁ × I.F.)dy + C.
Examples of linear DEs: dy/dx + y = sin x; dy/dx + (1/x)y = eˣ; dy/dx + (y/(x log x)) = 1/x.
Examples of Type 2: dx/dy + x = cos y; dx/dy + (-2x/y) = y²e⁻ʸ.
Example: dy/dx - y = cos x. P = -1, Q = cos x. I.F. = e⁻ˣ. Solution: y = (sin x - cos x)/2 + Ceˣ.
Example: x(dy/dx) + 2y = x². Rewrite: dy/dx + (2/x)y = x. P = 2/x, Q = x. I.F. = x². Solution: y = x²/4 + Cx⁻².
Example: y dx - (x + 2y²) dy = 0. Rewrite: dx/dy - x/y = 2y. P₁ = -1/y, Q₁ = 2y. I.F. = 1/y. Solution: x = 2y² + Cy.
Example: (tan⁻¹y - x) dy = (1 + y²) dx. Rewrite: dx/dy + x/(1+y²) = tan⁻¹y/(1+y²). I.F. = e^(tan⁻¹y). Solution: x = (tan⁻¹y - 1) + Ce^(-tan⁻¹y).

summary: An equation involving derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to independent variable (variables) is known as a differential equation.
Order of a differential equation is the order of the highest order derivative occurring in the differential equation.
Degree of a differential equation is defined if it is a polynomial equation in its derivatives.
Degree (when defined) of a differential equation is the highest power (positive integer only) of the highest order derivative in it.

A function which satisfies the given differential equation is called its solution. The solution which contains as many arbitrary constants as the order of the differential equation is called a general solution and the solution free from arbitrary constants is called particular solution.
Variable separable method is used to solve such an equation in which variables can be separated completely, i.e. terms containing y should remain with dy and terms containing x should remain with dx.

A differential equation which can be expressed in the form dy/dx = f(x, y) or dx/dy = g(x, y) where f(x, y) and g(x, y) are homogeneous functions of degree zero is called a homogeneous differential equation.
A differential equation of the form dy/dx + Py = Q, where P and Q are constants or functions of x only is called a first order linear differential equation.

exercises.title: Exercise 9.2
exercises.description: Verify that the given functions (explicit or implicit) is a solution of the corresponding differential equation.

questions.question: y = eˣ + 1 : y'' - y' = 0
questions.answer: Verified: y' = ex, y'' = ex, so y'' - y' = ex - ex = 0

questions.question: y = cos x + C : y' + sin x = 0
questions.answer: Verified: y' = -sin x, so y' + sin x = 0

questions.question: y = √(1 + x²) : y' = xy/(1 + x²)
questions.answer: Verified: y' = x/√(1 + x²) = xy/(1 + x²)

questions.question: y = Ax : xy' = y (x ≠ 0)
questions.answer: Verified: y' = A, so xy' = Ax = y

questions.question: y = x sin x : xy' = y + x√(x² - y²) (x ≠ 0 and x > y or x < -y)
questions.answer: Verified: y' = sin x + x cos x, substitution gives xy' = y + x|x cos x|

questions.question: xy = log y + C : y' = y²/(1 - xy) (xy ≠ 1)
questions.answer: Verified: Differentiating xy = log y + C gives y + xy' = y'/y, solving gives y' = y²/(1 - xy)

questions.question: y - cos y = x : (y sin y + cos y + x) y' = y
questions.answer: Verified: Differentiating y - cos y = x gives (1 + sin y)y' = 1, substitution confirms the equation

questions.question: x + y = tan⁻¹y : y² y' + y' + 1 = 0
questions.answer: Verified: Differentiating x + y = tan⁻¹y gives 1 + y' = y'/(1 + y²), which simplifies to y²y' + y² + 1 = 0

questions.question: y = √(a² - x²), x ∈ (-a, a) : x + y(dy/dx) = 0 (y ≠ 0)
questions.answer: Verified: y' = -x/√(a² - x²), so x + y·y' = x + √(a² - x²)·(-x/√(a² - x²)) = x - x = 0

questions.answer: y = 1 + Ae⁻ˣ (or equivalently, log|1 - y| = -x + C)

Find the equation of a curve passing through the point (0, 0) and whose differential equation is y' = eˣ sin x.
For the differential equation xy(dy/dx) = (x + 2)(y + 2), find the solution curve passing through the point (1, -1).

Find the equation of a curve passing through the point (0, -2) given that at any point (x, y) on the curve, the product of the slope of its tangent and y coordinate of the point is equal to the x coordinate of the point.

At any point (x, y) of a curve, the slope of the tangent is twice the slope of the line segment joining the point of contact to the point (-4, -3). Find the equation of the curve given that it passes through (-2, 1).
The volume of spherical balloon being inflated changes at a constant rate. If initially its radius is 3 units and after 3 seconds it is 6 units. Find the radius of balloon after t seconds.
questions.task: Find radius as function of time

questions.question: In a bank, principal increases continuously at the rate of r% per year. Find the value of r if Rs 100 double itself in 10 years (log_e 2 = 0.6931).
In a bank, principal increases continuously at the rate of 5% per year. An amount of Rs 1000 is deposited with this bank, how much will it worth after 10 years (e^0.5 = 1.648).

In a culture, the bacteria count is 1,00,000. The number is increased by 10% in 2 hours. In how many hours will the count reach 2,00,000, if the rate of growth of bacteria is proportional to the number present?
questions.task: Find time for bacteria count to reach 2,00,000

exercises.title: Miscellaneous Exercise on Chapter 9
exercises.description: Mixed problems covering all methods from the chapter.

questions.question:
For each of the differential equations given below, indicate its order and degree (if defined):
(i) d²y/dx² + 5x(dy/dx)² - 6y = log x
(ii) (dy/dx)³ - 4(dy/dx)² + 7y = sin x
(iii) d⁴y/dx⁴ - sin(d³y/dx³) = 0

For each of the exercises given below, verify that the given function (implicit or explicit) is a solution of the corresponding differential equation:
(i) xy = a eˣ + b e⁻ˣ + x² : x(d²y/dx²) + 2(dy/dx) - xy + x² - 2 = 0
(ii) y = eˣ(a cos x + b sin x) : d²y/dx² - 2(dy/dx) + 2y = 0
(iii) y = x sin 3x : d²y/dx² + 9y - 6 cos 3x = 0
(iv) x² = 2y² log y : (x² + y²)(dy/dx) - xy = 0
Prove that x² - y² = c(x² + y²)² is the general solution of differential equation (x³ - 3xy²) dx = (y³ - 3x²y) dy, where c is a parameter.
Find the general solution of the differential equation dy/dx + √((1 - y²)/(1 - x²)) = 0.
Show that the general solution of the differential equation dy/dx + (y² + y + 1)/(x² + x + 1) = 0 is given by (x + y + 1) = A(1 - x - y - 2xy), where A is parameter.
Find the equation of the curve passing through the point (0, π/4) whose differential equation is sin x cos y dx + cos x sin y dy = 0.
Find the particular solution of the differential equation (1 + e²ˣ) dy + (1 + y²) eˣ dx = 0, given that y = 1 when x = 0.
Solve the differential equation y e^(x/y) dx = (x e^(x/y) + y²) dy (y ≠ 0).
Find a particular solution of the differential equation (x - y)(dx + dy) = dx - dy, given that y = -1, when x = 0. (Hint: put x - y = t)
Solve the differential equation [e^(-2√x)/√x - y/√x](dx/dy) = 1 (x ≠ 0).
Find a particular solution of the differential equation dy/dx + y cot x = 4x cosec x (x ≠ 0), given that y = 0 when x = π/2.
Find a particular solution of the differential equation (x + 1)(dy/dx) = 2e⁻ʸ - 1, given that y = 0 when x = 0.
The general solution of the differential equation (y dx - x dy)/y = 0 is
The general solution of a differential equation of the type dx/dy + P₁x = Q₁ is
options: (A) y e^(∫P₁ dy) = ∫(Q₁ e^(∫P₁ dy)) dy + C
(B) y · e^(∫P₁ dx) = ∫(Q₁ e^(∫P₁ dx)) dx + C
(C) x e^(∫P₁ dy) = ∫(Q₁ e^(∫P₁ dy)) dy + C
(D) x e^(∫P₁ dx) = ∫(Q₁ e^(∫P₁ dx)) dx + C
questions.answer: (C) x e^(∫P₁ dy) = ∫(Q₁ e^(∫P₁ dy)) dy + C

questions.question: The general solution of the differential equation eˣ dy + (y eˣ + 2x) dx = 0 is
